page: Amazon Web Services m8id.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes dedicated CPU allocation with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance features 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps, managed via the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in single-core and multi-core operations. The Geekbench single-core score of 2208.0 and image processing score of 17618.0 place it in the top tier. Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 reaches 225.5 GB/sec, categorized as strong. In memory-intensive tasks, the instance achieves a read-only small block cached memory bandwidth of 2558.45 GB/sec, while large block uncached read bandwidth is 179.94 GB/sec. LLM inference benchmarks show strong prompt processing speeds of 2782.69 tokens/sec for a small 135M model and 218.35 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the inclusion of 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage directly on the instance eliminates the need for separate network-attached storage for high-throughput operations, optimizing resource density. However, the 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth may act as a bottleneck for highly distributed workloads that require massive inter-node communication. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, local database hosting, static web serving, and moderate machine learning inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers strong performance across various benchmarks, including a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2208.0 and an image processing score of 17618.0. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in text processing and single-threaded decompression. With a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps and integrated high-speed local storage, the m8id.8xlarge offers a balanced resource profile. This makes it highly suitable for database operations, web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 1900 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong CPU and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.8xlarge is a 32-vCPU general-purpose instance with 128 GB DDR5 RAM and 1900 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory, dedicated Intel Xeon processing, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and image processing benchmarks
Delivers 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Suitable for database hosting, web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference
